@copilot

The pull request is stuck in a conflict loop even though conflicts have already been resolved multiple times.

Please do the following precisely and in this order:

1. Re-check the PR branch against the current main branch and identify WHY GitHub still reports unresolved conflicts.
2. Resolve conflicts definitively by:
   - Keeping the corrected workflow path at .github/workflows/daily-update.yml only
   - Ensuring no duplicate or nested workflow files exist anywhere in the repo
   - Keeping the current tools/fetch_updates.py logic as implemented
   - Keeping the latest data/updates.json structure without reintroducing old versions
3. Ensure all conflict markers (<<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>>) are fully removed and committed.
4. Rebase or merge main into the PR branch ONCE and commit the resolved state.
5. Push the cleaned branch so GitHub no longer reports conflicts.

Do NOT introduce new functionality.
Do NOT refactor code.
Do NOT change behavior.
The goal is ONLY to end the conflict state so the PR becomes mergeable.

When finished, confirm explicitly that:
- GitHub shows “This branch has no conflicts”
- The PR can be merged without further resolution steps.
